seoTitle: South Murwillumbah vs South Pambula — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Murwillumbah and South Pambula. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Murwillumbah vs South Pambula: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Murwillumbah and South Pambula.
heading: South Murwillumbah vs South Pambula — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Murwillumbah, New South Wales and South Pambula, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Murwillumbah has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to South Pambula (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Murwillumbah or South Pambula safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Murwillumbah has a flood risk score of 57/100, while South Pambula scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Murwillumbah scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. South Pambula scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Murwillumbah or South Pambula?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Murwillumbah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
